creator: swell.k@gmail.com
text:
dougb@FreeBSD.org writes:

> Synopsis: [patch] ports-mgmt/portmaster: use %%PREFIX%%/etc for config file
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
> State-Changed-By: dougb
> State-Changed-When: Sun May 16 22:23:52 UTC 2010
> State-Changed-Why: 
>
> First, portmaster doesn't install /usr/local/etc/portmaster.rc, the user has
> to create it. So portmaster is not breaking any rules here.

This...

- implies that user must have write access to LOCALBASE and ignores
  INSTALL_AS_USER + user-writable PREFIX.

- makes impossible to use different config files with multiple
  installations when different PREFIX is used each time. portmaster
  currently doesn't provide an option to specify location of config
  file.

Please, reopen.

> Second, I'm not
> sure you understand the distinction between LOCALBASE and PREFIX. The former
> is where the local files are already installed on the system. The latter is
> where the port will install files with the current operation. 
>

This doesn't clarify what install here means. In pkg_install sense user
produced files (e.g config, db, log) are not usually installed. Some
ports use @exec/@unexec to handle configs, some are content with only
having .sample/example files around.

Porter's Handbook mentions only PREFIX/etc for config files.

>
> hope this helps,
>
> Doug
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=146627
